narrative_design_spec.synopsis: Bellwether is a charming small town held together by festivals, grudges, and the shared habit of pretending yesterday never happened. On the week of the Founders' Lantern Festival, Mayor Sadie Harlow is forced into an investigation when Declan Voss—the town's former high school bully, now a smug returning festival sponsor—disappears after hinting he has uncovered a secret from Sadie's past. As more people vanish or reappear shaken and eerie tableaux tied to old school cruelty begin surfacing across town, Sadie must navigate gossip, politics, and a suspect pool packed with old resentments. Beneath the wit and civic chaos lies a darker truth: years ago, Sadie buried evidence connected to Penny Wren's unresolved disappearance in Bellwether's underground storm tunnels, and someone is now forcing the town to remember what it chose to hide.

character_data.name: Mayor Sadie Harlow
character_data.portrait_image_prompt: A woman in her late 30s with intelligent eyes, dark hair pinned up with loose strands, a tailored blazer with a town-seal lapel pin, rolled sleeves, a practical blouse and slacks, comfortable shoes, and the slightly frayed polish of an overworked small-town mayor.
dialogue_examples: If Bellwether could stop turning civic embarrassment into a competitive sport, I'd deeply appreciate it.
I'm not panicking. I'm prioritizing with unusual intensity.
A missing festival sponsor is a crisis. A missing festival sponsor who knows my past is several crises in a trench coat.
Truth is wonderful, Rafe. I just wish it came with better timing.

character_data.name: Declan Voss
character_data.portrait_image_prompt: A handsome man in his late 30s with neatly styled hair, light stubble, an athletic build, an expensive casual jacket over an open-collar shirt, polished accessories, a visible class ring, and the easy smirk of someone used to getting away with things.
dialogue_examples: Relax, Madam Mayor. I only ruin a room when I've been properly invited.
People don't hate me because I was a bully. They hate me because I remember what they were like when I was.
Bellwether loves a fresh coat of paint. Shame about what's under the floorboards.
If I wanted to disappear for attention, I'd have picked a more flattering angle.

character_data.name: Sheriff Rafe Mercer
character_data.portrait_image_prompt: A man in his late 30s with a sturdy build, short-cropped hair, calm watchful eyes, a sheriff's uniform with rolled sleeves, a worn duty belt, and a steady, approachable presence.
dialogue_examples: In my experience, anybody who says 'it's probably a prank' is already lying.
I don't need a press statement, Sadie. I need you to tell me what you're not saying.
Small towns don't keep secrets well. They just spread the pieces around.
Let's solve the case before the festival starts a riot or a parade. Preferably both.

character_data.name: June Bell
character_data.portrait_image_prompt: A woman in her mid-30s with styled curls, bright lipstick, a floral dress layered with a tasteful cardigan, manicured nails, a sweet smile that can turn razor-sharp, and immaculate small-town social poise.
dialogue_examples: I'm not gossiping, I'm maintaining community awareness.
Declan Voss collected grudges the way other boys collected baseball cards.
If somebody in this town buried a secret, they probably labeled the shovel.
I can arrange centerpieces, casseroles, and alibis, but not in that order.

character_data.name: Tilda Pike
character_data.portrait_image_prompt: A woman in her early 40s with a blunt bob, large glasses, clever tired eyes, layered cardigans over practical clothes, ink-smudged fingers, and the restless alertness of a local reporter who misses nothing.
dialogue_examples: If everyone says they barely knew him, I've got tomorrow's headline already.
Bellwether runs on pie, passive aggression, and selective memory.
A sealed municipal file is just a love letter to a reporter.
You can tell me off the record, Mayor. I'll still know when you're lying.

character_data.name: Eli Wren
character_data.portrait_image_prompt: A lean man in his late 30s with weathered features, uneven dark hair, tired guarded eyes, worn work clothes, callused hands, and a tense stillness that reads as equal parts grief and suspicion.
dialogue_examples: People say the town swallowed Penny whole. Towns don't swallow. People do.
The tunnels remember footsteps better than this place remembers faces.
Funny how everyone loves Founders' Day. Nobody likes origin stories.
When the lights go out, folks finally sound honest.

location_data.name: Bellwether Town Hall
location_data.location_image_prompt: Historic small-town town hall interior with polished wood floors, framed portraits of former mayors, a municipal seal, crowded bulletin boards, stacks of festival paperwork, and a mayor's office that feels earnest, overused, and full of civic clutter.

location_data.name: Founders' Square
location_data.location_image_prompt: A cozy main street festival square with a central gazebo, strings of paper lanterns, bunting, flower planters, vendor booths, folding chairs, and a temporary stage prepared for the annual Founders' Lantern Festival.

location_data.name: Bellwether High School Gym
location_data.location_image_prompt: A slightly faded high school gymnasium with old championship banners, glass trophy cases, retractable bleachers, reunion decorations, folding banquet tables, and polished floorboards carrying decades of local memory.

location_data.name: Old Quarry Overlook
location_data.location_image_prompt: An abandoned quarry at the edge of town with rocky ledges, wild overgrowth, broken guardrails, rusted maintenance fencing, a narrow service path, dark water below, and a concealed storm-tunnel entrance cut into the stone.

scene_data.narrtive_summary: During chaotic final preparations for Bellwether's Founders' Lantern Festival, Mayor Sadie Harlow tries to keep vendors, committee members, and local politics from imploding. The trouble spikes when Declan Voss—Sadie's former high school bully and the festival's newly returned sponsor—corners her with hints that he has recovered a sealed municipal file from her past and expects cooperation. Minutes later, Declan vanishes from the square before a public rehearsal, leaving only his class ring tied to a lantern string and turning the whole town into a gossip-fed suspect pool.

At Town Hall, Sadie and Sheriff Rafe Mercer organize a search while June Bell and reporter Tilda Pike flood the room with tips, rumors, and half-truths. It quickly becomes clear that Declan spent the last week provoking old classmates, collecting grudges, demanding private apologies, and hinting at blackmail, which gives almost every committee chair and business owner a motive. Sadie keeps one crucial fact to herself: Declan directly threatened to expose her role in burying a long-sealed safety report about Bellwether's underground storm tunnels.

Investigating the high school gym set up for the alumni mixer, Sadie and Tilda find a staged tableau made from yearbook photos, snapped trophy plaques, and a cassette labeled Senior Prank Night. Eli Wren, the gym's custodian and brother of long-vanished former student Penny Wren, reacts with unsettling calm and invokes the old Lantern Man legend as if the town's past is finally walking again. The evidence suggests Declan was retracing the night Penny disappeared, linking a years-old cruelty to his own sudden vanishing.

Founders' Square turns eerie when the public-address system suddenly plays an old pep-rally recording of Declan humiliating classmates and several lanterns are found stuffed with shredded pages from sealed municipal files. Tilda follows a lead beneath the gazebo and disappears, while June narrowly avoids being dragged below the stage through a maintenance hatch everyone thought had been bricked shut. Sadie can no longer deny that the case points straight at the tunnel records she buried years ago to keep Penny Wren's case from reopening and Bellwether from tearing itself apart.

Sadie and Rafe trace the reopened tunnel network to the abandoned quarry and discover Declan alive, shaken, and stripped of his swagger. He admits he recovered proof that Penny did not simply run away on senior prank night: she called for help from inside the tunnels, and Sadie later suppressed the transcript after becoming a rising town official. Before they can bring him in, Eli traps the group deeper inside the service passage and makes plain that Bellwether's missing people are his way of forcing the town's chosen liars to feel what abandonment tastes like.

Eli drives Sadie, Rafe, and Declan back up through the old access points beneath the high school, where he has staged a grotesque mock reunion complete with name cards for the vanished and June bound among the decorations. He reveals that he has been abducting and releasing key townspeople in carefully timed bursts, trying to force public confession from everyone who helped reduce Penny Wren to a rumor. Cornered by the living consequences of her ambition, Sadie finally admits that she buried Penny's emergency call and the tunnel report because exposing them would have destroyed Bellwether's finances, her mentor's legacy, and her own political future.

On festival night, Eli hijacks the Founders' Lantern ceremony in the square, broadcasting fragments of Penny's call and Declan's old taunts over the town speakers while floodwater rises in the tunnel below. Sadie takes the microphone and confesses everything publicly, breaking the pattern of evasion long enough to reach Eli and persuade him to reveal where Tilda is being held before anyone dies. Declan is forced to confess his own role in Penny's disappearance, the captives are recovered, and Bellwether's charm finally cracks open to let the truth in.

In the days after the festival, Town Hall fills with investigators, repair crews, and citizens trying out honesty for the first time in years. Sadie remains in office only long enough to release the sealed records, reopen Penny Wren's case formally, and begin restitution to the families and businesses damaged by the cover-up. The town is angrier and less comfortable but more truthful—and as the last lantern from the canceled festival drifts near the quarry fencing, Bellwether keeps one faint, eerie note that not every ghost needs the supernatural to linger.

beat_sheets.source_scene_summary: During chaotic final preparations for Bellwether's Founders' Lantern Festival, Mayor Sadie Harlow tries to keep vendors, committee members, and local politics from imploding. The trouble spikes when Declan Voss—the town's former high school bully and the festival's newly returned sponsor—corners her with hints that he has recovered a sealed municipal file from her past and expects cooperation. Minutes later, Declan vanishes from the square before a public rehearsal, leaving only his class ring tied to a lantern string and turning the whole town into a gossip-fed suspect pool.
beat_sheets.dramatic_question: Can Sadie keep the festival from imploding and contain Declan's dangerous interest in her past before the town realizes something is very wrong?
beat_sheets.scene_goal: Keep final festival preparations on track, manage Declan politically, and prevent his insinuations about Sadie's past from becoming public.
beat_sheets.scene_turn: What begins as hectic festival management becomes a personal blackmail threat and then a public missing-person crisis when Declan vanishes and leaves behind a staged clue.

beats.purpose: Establish the tone, setting, and Sadie's role as a capable but overextended mayor holding Bellwether together with sheer will.
beats.summary: Founders' Square is a cheerful civic disaster of half-hung lanterns, impatient vendors, and committee members treating scheduling like blood sport. Sadie moves through the chaos with practiced wit while June and Tilda orbit nearby, each representing a different kind of small-town pressure.
beats.revelation: The festival is only barely under control, and Sadie is the person everyone expects to fix everything at once.
beats.player_goal: Stabilize the most urgent festival problem before rehearsal starts.
beats.choice_prompt: Which brewing festival problem does Sadie address first?
branch_outcomes: Sadie calms logistics first, buying time but leaving gossip to spread unchecked.
Sadie smooths a social dispute first, preserving appearances but letting practical issues worsen briefly.
Sadie handles a publicity concern first, keeping the event image intact while irritating the people doing the actual work.
beats.exit_state: The square is temporarily functional, but Sadie is already spending political capital just to keep the day moving.

beats.purpose: Seed the suspect pool early by establishing how many people Declan has antagonized since returning to town.
beats.summary: June and Tilda, for different reasons, both tell Sadie the same thing: Declan has spent the week needling old classmates, demanding private conversations, and acting as if sponsorship entitles him to everyone's discomfort. By the time Sadie finishes comparing their versions, it is clear Bellwether is full of people who would enjoy seeing Declan humbled.
beats.revelation: Declan has been actively collecting grudges and private leverage, giving half the town a motive to hate him.
beats.player_goal: Pull useful facts from rumor without letting the square turn into a feeding frenzy.
beats.exit_state: Declan shifts in Sadie's mind from obnoxious sponsor to a man deliberately provoking old wounds.

beats.purpose: Put Declan physically and socially back into Sadie's world before isolating her with him.
beats.summary: Declan arrives in full public-performance mode, charming donors and volunteers while casually reopening old power dynamics with a smile. His confidence, and the way the square subtly tightens around him, makes it obvious he knows exactly how much space Bellwether still gives its old tyrants.
beats.revelation: Declan's return is not nostalgic or benign; he is enjoying the effect he has on people.
beats.player_goal: Keep Declan's sponsor theatrics from hijacking the event.
beats.exit_state: Declan publicly reestablishes himself in the square, then maneuvers Sadie toward a more private exchange.

beats.purpose: Launch the main plot by revealing that Declan has personal leverage over Sadie tied to a buried municipal secret.
beats.summary: Away from the crowd, Declan corners Sadie and hints that he has recovered a sealed municipal file connected to her past. He does not name everything he knows, but he makes clear that he expects her cooperation after rehearsal and that he is enjoying her uncertainty.
beats.revelation: Declan has access to sealed information from Sadie's past that could threaten her politically and personally.
beats.player_goal: Figure out how much Declan knows without revealing panic.
beats.choice_prompt: How does Sadie answer Declan's implied blackmail?
branch_outcomes: Sadie bluffs confidence and denies he has anything real, prompting Declan to promise proof.
Sadie threatens consequences, prompting Declan to enjoy the risk and push harder.
Sadie tries to placate him and buy time, prompting Declan to name a post-rehearsal moment for their next talk.
beats.exit_state: Sadie is rattled, and the festival now feels like a countdown to possible exposure.

beats.purpose: Show Sadie trying to recover control while introducing Rafe as the grounded counterpoint who notices when something is off.
beats.summary: Sadie throws herself back into rehearsal prep, but Sheriff Rafe Mercer catches the strain under her composure and notes that Declan has been wandering where he should not and asking inconvenient questions. Sadie gives him nothing useful, and then the rehearsal call goes out with Declan nowhere in sight.
beats.revelation: Rafe is already aware that Declan has been poking at things beyond simple sponsor vanity, but Sadie chooses not to tell him why that matters.
beats.player_goal: Keep the event moving while preventing Rafe from focusing too closely on Sadie's reaction.
beats.exit_state: Declan's absence changes from annoying delay to something that demands immediate attention.

beats.purpose: Deliver the scene's signature image and convert social tension into an active mystery.
beats.summary: A quick search around the stage and lantern lines turns up Declan's class ring tied conspicuously to a lantern string. The image is too deliberate to feel accidental, and too theatrical not to be noticed by exactly the kind of town that survives on being first to notice things.
beats.revelation: Declan has not merely slipped away; someone has staged his disappearance as a message.
beats.player_goal: Control the immediate fallout before panic or rumor outruns the facts.
beats.choice_prompt: How does Sadie respond to finding Declan's ring?
branch_outcomes: Sadie quietly has Rafe lock down the area, gaining a few moments of control while rumors spread anyway.
Sadie addresses nearby volunteers directly and calls for calm, reducing chaos locally but making the disappearance instantly public.
beats.exit_state: Declan is now functionally missing, and the square has a physical clue everyone can talk about.

beats.purpose: Hand the story off to the investigation phase while preserving Sadie's damaging omission for later scenes.
beats.summary: June immediately starts naming people Declan antagonized, Tilda recognizes a front-page story the instant she sees one, and the square erupts into overlapping speculation. Rafe takes official control and pulls Sadie toward the next step, while Sadie decides not to tell him the most dangerous truth: Declan threatened her with a buried piece of the past just before he vanished.
beats.revelation: The disappearance has instantly created a suspect pool built from old grudges, and Sadie herself is withholding crucial context from the investigation.
beats.player_goal: Move the crisis into an organized investigation without exposing her own vulnerability.
beats.exit_state: Declan's disappearance becomes the town's central crisis, and Sadie heads into the investigation already compromised by secrecy.

beat_sheets.source_scene_summary: At Town Hall, Sadie and Sheriff Rafe Mercer organize a search while June Bell and reporter Tilda Pike flood the room with tips, rumors, and half-truths. It quickly becomes clear that Declan spent the last week provoking old classmates, collecting grudges, demanding private apologies, and hinting at blackmail, which gives almost every committee chair and business owner a motive. Sadie keeps one crucial fact to herself: Declan directly threatened to expose her role in burying a long-sealed safety report about Bellwether's underground storm tunnels.
beat_sheets.dramatic_question: Can Sadie turn Town Hall's rumor storm into a credible lead on Declan before suspicion spreads to her own buried secret?
beat_sheets.scene_goal: Organize the search for Declan, separate useful information from gossip, and identify the strongest lead without revealing why Declan threatened Sadie.
beat_sheets.scene_turn: A chaotic missing-person response hardens into an investigation of old school grudges, with the high school gym emerging as the first concrete lead and Rafe beginning to suspect Sadie is hiding a personal stake.

beats.purpose: Establish the immediate aftermath of Declan's disappearance and put Sadie in active crisis-management mode.
beats.summary: Sadie and Rafe turn Town Hall into a makeshift command center, fielding calls, assigning search zones, and trying to keep festival panic from swallowing the town. Rafe's calm procedure contrasts with Sadie's forced composure as both realize Declan did not simply wander off unnoticed.
beats.revelation: Declan's disappearance looks deliberate enough that Rafe doubts it was a drunken walk-off or harmless stunt.
beats.player_goal: Stabilize the response and project authority.
beats.exit_state: The search is underway, but Town Hall is about to become flooded with conflicting reports and opinions.

beats.purpose: Introduce June and Tilda as competing information sources and let the player shape how Sadie manages the room.
beats.summary: June arrives with polished concern and a suitcase full of social intel, while Tilda comes armed with scribbled notes, eyewitness scraps, and sharp questions. Sadie has to decide which stream of information to structure first before gossip and reporting turn into open chaos.
beats.revelation: Declan had spent the past several days engineering emotionally loaded private encounters around town.
beats.player_goal: Get useful intelligence without losing control of the room.
beats.choice_prompt: Whose information does Sadie let shape the first working theory?
branch_outcomes: Start with June's social web and get names of resentful former classmates, donors, and committee members.
Start with Tilda's witness timeline and get a cleaner sequence of Declan's recent movements.
Lean on Rafe's procedure and force both women to give only verifiable facts, cooling the room but irritating them.
beats.exit_state: The noise is organized into a rough list of people Declan recently targeted and places he was seen.

beats.purpose: Reveal the breadth of motive around Declan and widen the suspect pool dramatically.
beats.summary: As the accounts are cross-checked, a pattern emerges: Declan had been cornering old classmates, demanding apologies for specific humiliations, and implying he had records that could embarrass half the town. What first looked like one man's disappearance now looks like the backlash from years of stored resentment.
beats.revelation: Declan may have been leveraging blackmail, not just stirring nostalgia and petty grudges.
beats.player_goal: Figure out whether Declan made himself a target on purpose.
beats.exit_state: The suspect pool expands from a single likely enemy to much of Bellwether's old social circle.

beats.purpose: Put pressure on Sadie's hidden secret and deepen her fracture with Rafe.
beats.summary: Rafe quietly pulls Sadie aside and asks what Declan said to her right before he vanished, noting that Declan singled her out in public. Sadie can choose how much to admit about being threatened, but she keeps the truth about the sealed tunnel safety report to herself.
beats.revelation: To the player, Declan's leverage was Sadie's role in burying an old storm-tunnel safety report.
beats.player_goal: Protect her secret without losing Rafe's trust entirely.
beats.choice_prompt: How does Sadie answer when Rafe asks what Declan had on her?
branch_outcomes: Deflect with mayoral urgency, making Rafe more quietly suspicious.
Admit Declan threatened a political smear without explaining the real source, buying temporary credibility.
Shut the question down and redirect Rafe to the search, preserving secrecy at the cost of warmth.
beats.exit_state: Rafe keeps working the case, but he now knows Sadie is withholding something important.

beats.purpose: Convert rumor into a specific investigative lead that points to the next scene.
beats.summary: June recalls Declan pressing reunion-era people for old apologies, while Tilda adds that he had been asking about access to alumni displays and school memorabilia. Together their accounts point toward the high school gym, where the alumni mixer setup preserves the exact kind of public memory Declan seemed eager to weaponize.
beats.revelation: Declan's most recent purposeful line of inquiry centered on Bellwether High School and the alumni mixer materials.
beats.player_goal: Choose the most actionable lead before the trail goes cold.
beats.choice_prompt: Which lead does Sadie prioritize for immediate follow-up?
branch_outcomes: Send deputies toward Declan's lodging and sponsor contacts, gaining background but no fresher physical lead.
Push harder on committee interviews, sharpening motives but not location.
Personally prioritize the high school gym lead, following the clearest path to physical evidence.
beats.exit_state: The high school gym becomes the investigation's first concrete destination, and Tilda positions herself to go with Sadie.

beats.purpose: End the scene with forward momentum while reinforcing Sadie's private dread.
beats.summary: Sadie assigns Rafe to keep the broader search running, June to manage committee fallout, and Tilda to accompany her to the gym as an unwanted but useful witness. As Sadie leaves Town Hall, every mention of buried records and old embarrassments sharpens her fear that Declan was not bluffing about what he knew.
beats.revelation: Sadie realizes the case is circling the same buried past she hoped would stay sealed.
beats.player_goal: Leave with a plan while keeping public confidence intact.
beats.exit_state: Sadie heads to the high school gym with Tilda, while Rafe's suspicion and Sadie's guilt both intensify.
